verification.manifest: draft — Phase A skeleton per QMCP Blueprint V1.0, not yet reviewed by eff
verification.runtime: 13/13 executors implemented (Phase B deterministic evidence nodes + Phase C AI editorial nodes + Phase D current-issue research, wired to ReligiousSourceService/AiService/CurrentIssueResearchService in apps/api/src/execution/real-nodes/index.ts per QMCP Volume 2). discover_current_issues degrades to the honest RESEARCH_SERVICE_NOT_CONFIGURED stub whenever no approved RSS/news source is registered (CURRENT_ISSUE_RESEARCH_SOURCES) — the source allowlist is a content-governance decision for eff, same posture as the QUL dataset pick. Verified via sandbox tsc build against a real local HTTP fixture feed (not reimplemented logic). Not yet typechecked/tested on a live install (sandbox cross-mount symlink limitation — pending eff's Windows pnpm build:packs/typecheck/test pass).
verification.templates: draft — two importable templates. issue-to-dakwah-video: the main chain with all three human gates wired, plus a real Video Production handoff (prepare_media_brief -> read_script -> draft_scenes, via new scriptText/title ports) and a real human-facing Gate R on the revision branch (replacing a passive log). issue-to-dakwah-video-revision: companion template that re-runs only compose_reflection->write_short_video_script->validate_dakwah_content against an already Gate-2-approved bundle -- the execution engine is a strict DAG (verified via graph-planner.ts's Kahn's-algorithm cycle detection, CYCLE_DETECTED blocks the whole run), so a same-graph loop-back edge is not possible; this is the real substitute, re-triggerable by an operator. Phase F fix: validate_dakwah_content now also outputs a flat publicationBlocked boolean port (alongside validation) because cond-publication's expression grammar has no dot-path field access -- the original Phase E wiring (whole validation object into a single value port, expression referencing a nested field) would have thrown EXPRESSION_ERROR on every real run, caught by extracting and running the real compiled expression grammar during Phase F test-writing, not by inspection alone. Gate 3 and Gate R contexts are now wired directly from validate_dakwah_content.validation rather than through cond-publication (see templates/README.md). Both graphs re-verified structurally after the fix (zero cycles, zero unknown node types, zero port-id mismatches, every node reachable) via the real compiled graph-planner.ts against the real cross-pack port registry (workflow-foundation + human-work + video-production + quran-media). Also fixed: all four lados.human.request_input gate nodes were missing the inputSchema config field the executor requires (would have failed MISSING_INPUT on first pause), and every gate node's guidance text was under a dead 'instructions' key never read by the executor (renamed to 'description'). Optional hadith verification remains a standalone example node (see templates/README.md). Not yet sanity-checked with a real live import + trigger on eff's Windows install.

verification.manifest: verified — S9.1 fix: the skeleton declared "workflow.control.loop" and "workflow.event.publish" capabilities with no corresponding nodes; added lados.workflow.loop and lados.workflow.publish_event to close both gaps. "workflow.trigger.event" remains capability-only by design — event-triggered workflow starts are handled by EventBusService subscription dispatch (see event-bus.service.ts), not by a runnable node; a placeholder node would have no executable behavior of its own. Phase 22 S22.4 (2026-07-06): added "workflow.control.switch" + lados.workflow.switch (true multi-way routing, 5 fixed case slots + default — see switch.ts header for why fixed rather than dynamic) and extended lados.workflow.condition's expression grammar (named fields + AND/OR combinator, backward compatible). Phase 23 S23.3 (2026-07-08): added "workflow.pipeline.save_artifact"/"workflow.pipeline.read_artifact" + lados.workflow.pipeline_save_artifact/pipeline_read_artifact — cross-workflow data handoff scoped to one pipeline_run_id (migration 0075's pipeline_artifacts table), successor in spirit to the dead project.save_artifact/project.read_artifact (archived with lados.core-pack in Phase 21 S9) but architecturally new, not a resurrection. Phase 24 S24.3 (2026-07-11): renamed to "workflow.program.save_artifact"/"workflow.program.read_artifact" + lados.workflow.program_save_artifact/program_read_artifact — corporate-vocabulary rename (Pipeline→Program), same table/column targets as migration 0079 renamed them to (program_run_id, program_artifacts). Old node type strings preserved as compatibility aliases, see @lados/pack-sdk's compatibility-aliases.ts.
verification.runtime: implemented — 12 of 13 capabilities have a backing node (see manifest note above for the one intentional exception, workflow.trigger.event)

workflows.summary: End-to-end QMCP chain: discover and rank current issues, Gate 1 human issue selection, impact analysis, Islamic theme mapping, QUL Quran + tafsir evidence retrieval, evidence bundle, Gate 2 religious evidence review, reflection, short-video script, content validation, publication-blocked condition, Gate 3 editorial approval, and media brief. Optional hadith verification and the Video Production handoff nodes are included standalone (see templates/README.md). See issue-to-dakwah-video.workflow.json for the importable graph body — note every QMCP executor is a Phase A stub, so a live run will stop at discover_current_issues with RESEARCH_SERVICE_NOT_CONFIGURED until Phases B–D wire the services.

Companion template to issue-to-dakwah-video for the Blueprint §14 'Revision path' after a validate_dakwah_content block. The main execution engine is a strict DAG (cycle detection blocks any workflow containing a real loop-back edge — verified in packages/execution-engine/src/graph-planner.ts), so this pack cannot wire a literal loop back into the main template's graph. This template is the real, re-triggerable substitute: a human resubmits the SAME already Gate-2-approved evidence bundle (no need to repeat issue discovery or either evidence review), and only the AI-authored content stage (compose_reflection → write_short_video_script → validate_dakwah_content) re-runs. If validation still blocks publication, Gate R fires again and the operator can re-trigger this same template as many times as needed — an operator-driven loop across separate runs, not a same-graph cycle. See issue-to-dakwah-video-revision.workflow.json for the importable graph body.
Create a fleet job, complete operational evidence, submit invoice, verify invoice, and record human approval.
Dispatch a trip, record completion, capture exceptions, and create follow-up tasks where required.
Upload a fuel receipt, extract receipt data, route low-confidence or policy-sensitive claims for human expense approval.
Create a maintenance record, assign follow-up work, request procurement where needed, and record clearance.
Prepare a payroll run, request human review, record approval, and notify payroll stakeholders.
Read a BOQ, classify trades, enrich with approved rate references, and route the cost summary for review.
Register notice evidence, link contract references, prepare QS valuation checks, and preserve human commercial review.
Gather contract sums, variations, payments, retention, and evidence into a final account review workflow.
Prepare chronology, evidence register, notice references, claim summaries, and review checkpoints for a CIPAA preparation bundle.
Log a defect, create a task/case, notify responsible parties, track reminders, and record human closeout.
Submit invoice, verify invoice references, request human approval, record decision, and notify finance stakeholders.
Create an RFQ, parse quotations, compare compliant offers, route award recommendation for human review, and request PO creation.
Collect site evidence, assess claim references, flag missing evidence, and route QS review before certification.

Trigger a run, read a script, draft a numbered scene plan, lock the first creative-direction batch, and request a render — plus three ad-hoc example nodes (revise layout, chroma-key validation, image insertion) showing their config contract standalone, since their single-scene shape doesn't auto-chain off the bulk scene array. See script-to-scene-plan.workflow.json for the real, importable graph body.

rankedBlockers.reason: Descriptor-only L3/L5 assets cannot prove node, service, resource, connector, or port dependencies and cannot be imported as runnable workflows.
Official configGroups declare keys only, so the API derives optional string fields instead of typed, validated, resource-, knowledge-, or connection-aware controls.
Declared stubs (lados.communication.send_sms, lados.document.read_pdf, lados.document.read_docx, lados.video.render_scenes) block any graph that requires their real behavior.
No live official L4 pack exists; provider selection must follow workflow graph completion rather than assumptions.
lados.communication, lados.document-intelligence, lados.quran-media, lados.video-production declare or derive degraded readiness and require explicit service/configuration verification.

firstActivationWave: Complete the 13 missing workflow graph bodies before using L3/L5 assets to choose connectors.
Activate Document Intelligence first: implement PDF/DOCX extraction and document-library persistence because document handling is a dependency of multiple solution/template packs.
Finish typed configuration for Workflow Foundation, Human Work, Document Intelligence, Communication, and the professional packs used by the first graph bodies.
Add production-strict missing-executor behavior before certifying any workflow.
connectorDecision: Do not select Microsoft 365 versus Google Workspace from descriptor prose alone.
After graph bodies exist, extract exact triggers/actions and score provider demand across the prepared workflows.
Generic HTTP/webhook, file/attachment, connection profile, OAuth, retry, pagination, and rate-limit foundations remain provider-neutral prerequisites.
firstCertificationSet: Author and validate lados.solution.contractor_ops.trip_dispatch_and_completion first because its declared dependencies (Asset Fleet and Task Case) are both statically runtime-ready and contain no declared stubs.
Use lados.solution.contractor_ops.fleet_maintenance as the second business graph because Asset Fleet, Task Case, and Procurement are statically runtime-ready.
Video Production script-to-scene-plan is graph-backed but degraded by lados.video.render_scenes; certify an inspection/planning-only variant or implement the render backend before full certification.
Quran Media main and revision workflows are graph-backed and statically resolvable; they require configuration/service and live import/run verification rather than graph authoring.
Begin QS graph authoring after Document Intelligence PDF/DOCX and storage gaps are resolved, because all three QS Practice descriptors depend on that degraded pack.

adHocFindings: Phase 27 was correctly numbered after existing Phase 25 and reserved Phase 26 plans were discovered.
Production-strict execution and resolver-backed API readiness landed in S27.1; this report mirrors the same state vocabulary for build-time checks.
The Quran Media source header still describes all nodes as stubs while nodes.json and the manifest now declare 13 implemented executors; documentation/runtime comments have drifted.
Composition descriptors validate required pack IDs but do not declare the path to a workflow body, even where sibling workflow JSON exists; matching currently relies on filename convention.
Direct spec files exist for every executable pack, but direct spec presence alone is not proof of provider or live E2E readiness.
limitations: This report does not read environment files and does not evaluate credentials or secrets.
Build evidence is static API import wiring plus node-type declaration; the runtime API additionally probes the live resolver factory.
Test evidence is filename-based. S27.1/S27.5 should add machine-readable test evidence or probe results.
Generic configuration readiness is inferred from the current official loader, which derives string fields from configGroups.
Provider readiness cannot be certified without sandbox/test accounts and real round trips.
Supabase migration/application state and browser import/run behavior are outside this static baseline.
